text: Who do you think brought Septimus here? Who do you think protected you on your journey to open the box and loose my knowledge on this world?
Your free will is an illusion. Whether you acknowledge me or not is your own business. But I will be in your mind.
An Amulet of Mara? I haven't seen such a thing in a very long time.
You go ahead and arrange the wedding ceremony when you're ready.
You've embarrassed the temple and spat in the face of love. What do you want?
Is that an Amulet of Mara? I'm surprised someone like you isn't spoken for.
An Amulet of Mara? You're not married? Surprising.
That amulet. I've seen the Nords wear these when they're looking to marry. With your strength, you should have no trouble.
Is that Lady Mara's amulet? I'm shocked a fine person like you isn't married already.
You're wearing Mara's amulet? You? I'd marry you. I'd marry you twice.
Look at you. Wearing an Amulet of Mara. Are you saying you're available?
An Amulet of Mara, you're looking for marriage, then?
You should arrange the ceremony at the Temple of Mara right away. I can't wait.
Have you spoken to the Temple of Mara, yet? I'm so excited.
You should speak to the priest in the Temple of Mara to arrange the ceremony. No sense waiting.
I'll see you at our wedding!
Hey, shouldn't you tell the Temple of Mara that we're getting married? Be hard to have a wedding without a priest.
Now shouldn't you be off arranging our wedding day? Don't keep me waiting.
You should tell the priest we're getting married. They'll take things from there.
Never thought I'd get married under a Nord god. But with you, it feels right.
Our wedding is soon. I'll see you then!
We have our wedding soon, don't we? That's right, we do!
Our wedding is soon, under Mara's loving eyes. It'll be perfect.
I'm looking forward to our wedding. You and me....
The wedding is soon. It'll be a good day.
I'm the best warrior in Morthal, and that's no boast.
As you wish.
As you wish.
Your call.
Hoo jah.
Suit yourself.
Right, then.
Bafagra badee.
I am watching, Skaal-friend.
Go on, then.
Still have something you need?
Hafahoo?!
Is there still something you need of me?
What was it you needed, again?
Yes, Thane. What is it?
Takafala gajoo?
What would you ask of me?
Just let me know what needs doing.
I'm sorry, but I'm not going to do something like that. Even for you.
Joooo fah!
Anything else before you go?
Kawafoo.
I'm sorry, that can't be done.
No problem, I'll handle it.
Araa.
Did you need something?
Baroo?
Come on. Let's see what you're made of.
I am Hermaeus Mora. I have been watching you since you defeated Miraak. Your progress is most impressive.
I am Hermaeus Mora. I have been watching you since we last spoke, and I am pleased with your progress.
I am Hermaeus Mora. I am the guardian of the unseen, and knower of the unknown. I have been watching you, mortal.
Most impressive.
Got this from a pretty excited looking dark elf around Raven Rock. He said it was important I got it to you as soon as I could.
Another note from your friend Ralis. I hope everything is all right.
Another letter from Ralis. He's starting to scare me a little.
I've got another letter from Ralis, but this is the last thing I'm delivering for him. That place is just too much for me.
Come on, you monster!
Come on, you monster!
Skyrim belongs to the Nords!
Fine! I'll take care of it, just please leave me alone!
Ugh. You mangy piece of pit-bait.
I'd best get back to Master Neloth then. No telling what's happened since I've been gone.
I'm heading home if you need me.
Faaa rawala.
I need to get back to the village. My father will have need of me.
(hiss)
Well, then, I'm going to head back and get myself a drink. Maybe I'll see you soon.
It has been an honor, Skaal-friend.
What do you need to take?
Habichaaalakawaramalek.
Very well.
Whatever you say, boss.
Now that's good work. Everyone will be a lot safer with them gone, Dark Elf and Nord alike.
Here, some gold as promised, and let me show you a bit about wearing heavy armor. Save you some aches on a long march.
Your kindness warms my heart as sunlight warms the skin on a cold day. The Skaal are once again in your debt, outsider.
Yes. It is.
I was foolish to expect compassion from an outsider.
That's a brave offer. I'd be happy to throw in my coin behind that.
You deal with them, I'll make sure you get paid. Maybe this old soldier will throw in some lessons as well.
Then may the All-Maker give you the strength of a great bear and make your will as firm as stone.
Whenever a group of marauders attack a Nord village, Ulfric is the first to sound the horn and send the men.
But a group of Dark Elf refugees gets ambushed? A group of Argonians, or a Khajiit caravan? No troops. No investigation. Nothing.
There's a group of cutthroats out there right now that Ulfric doesn't lift a finger to bring to justice, as long as they don't threaten Nord land.
Bandits sometimes come at night to steal our food. They think that stealing from us is easier than hunting for themselves.
It will be dangerous, but if you'll deal with these thieves, we would be most grateful.
I used to be an adventurer in my day, then I settled down. Got married.
Have you put those thieving bandits to the sword?
Won't lie, I am. And you?
I cannot deny it. You remind me of all the things I miss about Skyrim, and my heart beats a little quicker when you are near. Do you feel the same?
Lead the way.
I will shadow your steps.
Sure thing, friend.
I owe you that much.
Waaaaah!
Waaaaah!
(hiss)
Go on ahead. I'll stand guard.
Waaaah!
If that is what you wish.
(hiss)
Right, then.
Take a look.
I gathered some plants that are useful for making potions, but I have other goods as well.
Among other things, I have some fresh bread I baked this morning and vegetables from Finna's garden.
Mostly things from the village or the land around it. Have a look.
Show those marauders what Windhelm justice tastes like.
Lead the way.
Then lead on, friend.
I'm right behind you.
You already appear to have someone with you.
Milk drinker.
Come back when you have the coin.
Want me to prove it? I bet a hundred gold I can take you, bare-handed.
Not so tough now, are you?
I think I can teach you a thing or two.
I'll show you what I can about protecting yourself.
I'll gladly share my knowledge with you.
It's all in the swing. Let me show you.
Not recently. Neloth keeps me very busy with spell research.
Ask Elynea. She and Varona seem to be quite friendly.
Is it tea time already?
Well, Neloth's just going to have to wait. Varona went to Raven Rock to arrange for supplies.
She left for Raven Rock hours ago. She promised to bring me back some fresh blisterwort.
I don't care where she is. Go get her. Now.
Any my tea better be hot.
Have you seen Varona? I'm hungry.
Have you found Varona yet? I could really use some tea.
When you find my new steward, have him report to me. With a cup of canis root tea.
Back so soon?
It's a shame about Varona.
I can't believe Varona is dead.
Find a new steward for us quickly. Master Neloth is making me do all her work.
I'm off to take up my new position with Master Neloth.
Neloth may be crazy, but it's better than staying in Raven Rock.
What! Who's going to make my tea?
Why do these things always happen to me?
Well, don't just stand there. Go find me another steward.
I'm sure there's plenty of people in Raven Rock to choose from.
Well find her. Tell her I want apple cabbage stew, with some canis root tea.
Hmm. I might have a few staffs, if you're interested in that sort of thing.
Mostly staffs. They're my specialty.
I suppose I could dig up a staff or two for you.
I've got the best selection of mushroom and fungi.
I probably have some potions around here that might interest you.
Every type of fungus in Solstheim or Skyrim. Oh, and a few other ingredients and potions.
All of my people have left to work on that sacred stone. It's quite fascinating. Annoying, but fascinating.
Things have been much calmer since you disposed of Ildari.
Do you need all your toes? I could really use a fresh toe for...nevermind. You aren't moldy enough.
It's good to have everyone not running off to that damn sacred stone every night. I suppose I have you to thank for that.
Thank you for rescuing me from High Point tower.
I still have nightmares about what Ildari did to my friends.
If you're going to be up here, stay out of my way.
Where is that ash sucking steward of mine? Varona!
Where is that lazy steward of mine? Varona! No, wait. She's dead. Drovas!
My new steward is...acceptable. If only he made better tea.
Everything that can go wrong is going wrong. Ash spawn attacks, my house, and even my steward is assassinated.
It's as if Tel Mithryn is cursed. Spoiled food, ash spawn attacks, my servants constantly getting sick. What's next?
How am I supposed to get anything done with part of the tower withered like this?
I wonder if a dragon could be captured alive? It would make a fascinating test subject.
Hmmm?
Why am I here? Did Master Neloth do one of his experiments on me again?
I hope Master Neloth can explain this.
I don't think you belong here. Does Master Neloth know about you?
Sometimes I feel sorry for them. Master Neloth's experiments tend to be fatal.
Shh! Master Neloth is concentrating. At least I think he is. He might be sleeping with his eyes open again.
It's nice to see a friendly face around here. Everyone else can be so grumpy.
Give me a moment. I still have flashback from when Master Neloth changed you.
Those poor spriggans. Ever since you healed the tower, Master Neloth has been experimenting on them.
Somehow Master Neloth found out about the Ash Guardian. I'm terrified of what he's going to do.
Thank you for keeping that matter of the Ash Guardian just between us. I shudder to think what would happen if Master Neloth had found out.
Master Neloth was furious when the tower withered. It just seemed to happen overnight.
Sometimes being Master Neloth's apprentice is...painful.
Dragons. Master Neloth says they are no match for a Telvanni wizard. But what about a mere apprentice?
One day I'll inherit his power. It will all be mine. Mark my words.
He never sleeps. I don't know how he does it, but he never rests for even a moment.
That whole thing with the standing stone was very odd. I'd just as soon forget about it.
Who brought me here? They better not have touched my mushrooms. Neloth? Neloth! Is this your doing?
This just isn't right. Why was I working on that stone?
Hmmph. Another wanderer. I suppose you'll be wanting potions, just like the others.
You'd think Neloth was still a clan leader in House Telvanni the way he carries on.
Mushrooms and molds of Vvardenfell have adapted to the ash far better than the green plants.
That ash eating wizard is always on me about his tower. It's not my damn fault the thing withered overnight.
I like it here at Tel Mithryn. Hardly anyone bothers me. People can be so irritating.
It's almost time to feed the mushrooms. They like a drop of blood every now and then you know.
My back still hurts from all that stone work. Why were we at that standing stone anyway?
Who brought me here? I demand to know who did this!
I've got to get back. Tel Mithryn is probably falling apart.
We don't get many visitors. Is there something you need?
I hate this ash. It gets into everything.
Thank you for rescuing us from that awful stone pillar.
There are rumors of dragons on the mainland. I hope they don't show up here.
I'm going to have to go to Raven Rock soon. We're running low on some things.
Talvas says Master Neloth is asking for me.
I hope Elynea has something for headaches.
I'd better check on Ulves. The kitchen supplies were getting low.
Oh dear. Why am I out here? I need to get back and make Master Neloth's tea.
This is just dreadful. Dreadful.
I hope you're not expecting a meal. I've barely got enough for them that live here.
I'd better tell Varona to bring apples and cabbage from Raven Rock.
Damnable ash! No matter how much I wash the food, it still gets in it.
Still can't figure out why I was working on that damned pillar.
Who did this to us? It isn't another one of Master Neloth's games, is it?
I... I must have been drugged. That's it. Someone drugged me. Maybe one of Mogrul's cronies.
Working on those standing stones feels like a bad dream. I'm glad you woke us from it.
After what you did for me, you are always welcome here. Just try to stay out of Master Neloth's way.
I heard Mogrul is dead. That is a relief!
I heard you paid my debt to Mogrul. I don't know how I can every repay you.
I just can't seem to get the hang of making a decent cup of canis root tea.
I heard you fought a dragon. That must have been terrifying.
I hope Elynea has something to help me sleep at night.
I can't thank you enough for setting me up as steward of Tel Mithryn.
Master Neloth certainly keeps me busy. Taking care of this estate is a lot of work.
It was fascinating to watch those tentacles grow out of your eyes.
Now that my tower is healed, I can finish some of my more delicate experiments.
How do you like my new laboratory? I use it to dissect spriggans. I've learned so much from them.
Talvas, I think I'll start with the burnt spriggan. They don't squirm as much.
First it's ash spawn attacks. Then my home withers. It's almost like someone is out to get me.
The ash from Red Mountain holds secrets. Secrets I mean to uncover.
Miraak was a formidable opponent. I wonder what he would have been like as my apprentice.
Defeating Miraak was an impressive feat. I'm curious to see if Hermaeus Mora seeks you out because of it.
That Drovas man?
I suppose he'll do. He makes dreadful canis root tea though.
But, I should be grateful. Here, take this for your efforts.
Oh, and you may use my staff enchanter if you want.
Well, I've never been one to pass up an opportunity.
Sorry to run, but I'm off to my new job.
It would have to be someone pretty desperate.
You might ask my apprentice Drovas. Lately he seems to be hard up for coin.
You could try Drovas Relvi.
He came in here asking my father if he needed any odd jobs done. Seemed kind of desperate.
As one Thieves Guild member to another? Try Drovas over in the Retching Netch.
Not too long ago he tried to pawn some stuff off. Seemed kinda desperate for coin.
Uh... you mean leave Raven Rock? For good? To go work for a crazed Telvanni wizard?
Sure. Just let me get my things.
On second thought, nevermind. I can get new stuff.
Are you nuts?
Neloth is crazy! I hear he talks to mud crabs.
Don't be ridiculous. Who would run this place?
You should ask around at The Retching Netch.
Someone there might be crazy enough, or drunk enough, to do it.
I'm perfectly content with my current position.
I could never leave Adril behind, and he will never leave Lleril.
Besides, I'd never work for Neloth! I heard he cut the heart out of his former apprentice.
Ha ha ha! Gods, that's rich. Work for Neloth. Ha ha ha!
My father needs me. He can't run his market stand alone.
Besides, Neloth is as loony as a skooma addict. He wanted my father to import a white mammoth. Alive!
Nah. I don't think so.
Anyway, I don't think he'd take on anyone that isn't a dunmer.
Me? I hardly think so.
That Telvanni wizard is unhinged. I heard he tries to drink the ash.
Now why would I want to work for House Telvanni?
Besides, Neloth is a madman!
Ha, ha, ha! Not on your life!
I'd have to be truly desperate to do that.
Not on your life! I'd sooner try to swim back to Windhelm.
I'd saw off my own leg for a septim, but you couldn't pay me enough to do that.
Would he give me skooma? I might do it for some skooma.
And leave my farm? No thank you.
Don't take too long finding me a steward.
That woman is never around when you need her.
Now where did I put...Oh, yeah.
The Raven Rock road is dangerous. I hope nothing happened to Varona.
Varona goes to Raven Rock all the time. I'm sure she's fine.
Dead? Well that's annoying.
How did she die?
I don't know what you did in those mines, but it put a smile on Crescius's face. I've never seen him so happy.
Allow me to apologize for Crescius's behavior, I'm afraid he hasn't been himself lately.
Don't be put off by my husband's mood... he enjoys having visitors.
It's been tough running the household alone, but Crescius isn't getting any younger.
Those East Empire people should be ashamed of themselves for abandoning Raven Rock.
Your contribution to our community will never be forgotten. Azura protect you, serjo.
Vendil Severin. A pleasure to meet you, serjo.
Always glad to help, serjo. What can I do for you?
We meet again, serjo. Speak your mind.
You've brought so much hope to our people, we are forever in your debt.
My husband Vendil feared visitors would shun Raven Rock. I'm glad to see he was mistaken.
Always nice to speak to someone from the mainland.
I hope you enjoy your stay in Raven Rock.
Mmm. Always a pleasure to watch when a man like you walks by.
It gets lonely out here you know, on this remote island.
Oh, hello. You looking for a handout or something?
My father and mother are the richest on the island... and that includes Councilor Morvayn.
If you ask me, my father knows how to run things better than the Councilor.
I'm free! Thank you, outlander.
If I could just get my hands on Miraak for what he did to us...
I'd been waking every morning with my hands scrapped and raw and now I know why. I appreciate what you've done for me.
Your actions here have been a blessing to us all, serjo.
Welcome to Raven Rock, traveler. Could I interest you in any fine ash-grown foods?
My wife and I are doing the best we can to keep Raven Rock fed.
If you ever need apothecary supplies, talk to my wife Milore.
My wife's quite the alchemist. Thanks to her, my ash yams are growing quicker than ever before.
You sure showed Miraak who's in charge around here!
Just thinking that some madman was inside my mind. Gives me shivers just thinking about it.
Just thinking that some madman was inside my mind. Gives me shivers just thinking about it.
I don't know who sent you here, but you've given us something to build on here. I hope someday we can repay you for your kindness.
Destroying the ash spawn has made it safer to work in the fields. Thank you.
Looking for any potions? Ingredients? Alchemical supplies?
If you're looking for any exotic ingredients, just ask. I might have what you need.
My husband Garyn maintains our farm. Without him, Raven Rock would likely go hungry.
I'm asking myself why Miraak was making us build these shrines... and I'm afraid the answer will be far worse than any of us can imagine.
That fetcher... making us build his shrines. If I could only get my hands on him...
Because of you, my life has meaning again. Thank you!
Spare some coin for a lost soul?
Anything you can offer, please. I'm starving.
Head back to the mainland, wanderer. There's nothing here but misery.
I lost my house, my family, my life... and then I lost my mind. How could it have gotten any worse?
The voices in my head are gone now thanks to you. Now I can finally get some rest.
Look at me, I'm a different person. And I have you to thank for it.
Don't pity me. And don't look at me like that. I know what I am.
I could swing a pickaxe better than any man in the mines. Made thousands of coins. Now it's all gone.
How are you supposed to live when your only means of trade is yanked out from under you?
Seeking passage to Windhelm?
Seeking passage to Solstheim?
On behalf of House Redoran and as Councilor of Raven Rock, I bid you welcome.
The mines are open and the ebony has begun to flow because of your discovery. My eternal gratitude and blessings go with you, friend.
I feel I owe you my life for thwarting the House Hlaalu plot to have me assassinated. My deepest thanks.
Traveling beyond The Bulwark will be much safer as the result of your victory at Fort Frostmoth. Many thanks.
Your contributions have earned you citizenship here, friend. I'll always consider you one of us.
Anything you can contribute to Raven Rock will be greatly appreciated... and rewarded appropriately, of course.
Don't let the state of Raven Rock fool you. Our desire for Raven Rock's return to its former glory is as strong as The Bulwark itself.
Whether you're simply visiting or staying here, I think you'll find our citizens will welcome you with open arms.
Second Councilor Adrano can assist you with anything you might need.
I can't thank you enough for reopening the ebony mines. You've brought light to the darkness, my friend.
You've succeeded where I've failed by ending the threat to Councilor Morvayn's life. I shall not forget it.
Assisting the Redoran Guard with the ash spawn attacks has earned you quite the reputation here. Keep it up.
My apologies for doubting you, serjo.
Your visit here will be tolerated as long as you abide by our laws, otherwise you'll answer to the Redoran Guard.
I've sworn to keep Councilor Morvayn safe, and I intend to take that oath to the grave if need be.
Most exiled dark elves call Vvardenfell their home. I call Raven Rock our home... may it last a thousand years.
I can't tell you how relieved I am that Miraak's gone from my mind. I appreciate what you've done.
I can't believe Miraak made us build those shrines. To take our free will like that... just horrible.
Ugh, I can't believe I was forced to do manual labor at those shrines. It was horrifying...
You've brought Raven Rock back from the brink, traveler. I don't know how to thank you.
Your efforts in Raven Rock have proven most invigorating for my husband. I haven't seen him this pleased in many years.
As the wife to the Second Councilor of Raven Rock, it would be my pleasure to help you any way that I can.
You aren't here with bad news regarding my husband, are you? I couldn't bear it.
It's been a long time since I walked the ashlands of Vvardenfell. I miss it terribly.
Perhaps our town isn't as grand as Mournhold, but it's our home and we must make the most of it.
With the ash spawn threat taken care of, I can give my men the rest they deserve. Well done, friend.
Modyn Veleth, Captain of the Redoran Guard at your service.
The security of both Raven Rock and Councilor Morvayn is my primary concern.
Looking for something to lend a hand with? We have problems aplenty in Raven Rock.
Don't let my posting here fool you. I was once in command of over a hundred troops... my luck simply changed when our homeland was destroyed.
Some consider my methods harsh, but playing the coddling nursemaid has never won a battle.
You honor the Reclamations with your actions. This will not be forgotten.
I am Othreloth, Elder of the Reclamations and keeper of this Temple.
Though our spirits may be low, and danger lurks beyond the light, I am confident that They will never abandon us again.
The Reclamations herald the dawn of a new age, and the downfall of the false Tribunal.
If you're in need of anything material, Attendant Hlervu can assist you.
You may have everyone fooled, but I see what you're doing here. The call of gold sends you scurrying like a rat.
Excuse me, but the Temple is for followers of our faith alone. Outsiders aren't welcome here.
Elder Othreloth is grooming me to be his eventual replacement. Soon, I'll be running the Temple my way.
If they desire salvation, the citizens of Raven Rock need to open their eyes... and their pockets.
Welcome to the Retching Netch Cornerclub, home of the finest sujamma that will ever grace your lips.
The mine's open, people are buying drinks and I'm making money again... just like old times.
I hear you're the reason Councilor Morvayn's still breathing today. Serves those damn Hlaalu traitors right!
How about a drink for the hero who cleared Fort Frostmoth!
Welcome to the Retching Netch, outlander. What can I get you?
If you're looking for a room, or perhaps some libations, you've come to the right place.
I know everyone in Raven Rock, and they know me. You want stories? I've got plenty.
I've seen many visitors come and go, but you... there's something about you that I like.
If I could only get my hands on Miraak. I'd make him pay for playing with my mind.
It's busier now that things are starting to look up. I guess I might stay a bit longer after all.
Being freed from building the shrine... I don't even know how to begin to thank you.
Want to buy anything, or just laugh at our misfortunes?
I'd be on the first ship off of this island if I had the coin. What a waste.
Yeah, so. What can I get you? Got everything from potions to pitchforks.
If you're looking to buy, then I'm looking to sell.
I know you risked a lot freeing me from Miraak's control. Thank you!
What if Miraak decides to make us do something else next... like kill someone we love. I can't bear the thought!
I can't believe that I was building that shrine at night... I never knew.
The mines are open and the shop is busy again. Thank you!
Miserable... just miserable. I hate this place.
This island's nothing but a pile of rubble. What's the point trying to make a life here?
You stuck in Raven Rock too? Horrible isn't it.
If you're looking to purchase something, speak to my father, Fethis.
Stealing a man's mind... it just isn't right. Thanks for saving me from that madman.
You're a coward, Miraak! Why don't you come out and face us yourself instead of controlling our minds!
I've heard of stealing things from people, but never thought you could steal someone's mind.
You've got a good thing going here now that you've reopened the mines. Don't let it go to waste.
Glover Mallory's the name. If you're looking for a smith, you've found one of the best.
Either you're here to get showered in sparks, or you're looking for something to buy. Out with it.
Steel, dwarven, ebony... if it's broken, I can mend it.
A lot of folks around here look to strangers for help. I hope you won't disappoint them.
I can't believe you found a way to reopen the mines. I don't know how to thank you.
There was a time when this town meant something. Now it's all gone.
For all I know, I might be the last Imperial left on Solstheim.
If it wasn't for my wife, Aphia, I'd have joined my ancestors long ago.
Maybe I won't live as long as the dark elves, but at least I didn't abandon my home.
Welcome to the Retching Netch Cornerclub, milady. If you need anything, Master Geldis will be happy to help you.
Welcome to the Retching Netch Cornerclub, milord. If you need anything, Master Geldis will be happy to help you.
We have all sorts of authentic dark elf food and drink here. Geldis spares no expense.
Geldis may be a tough boss, but I can put up with it. Keeps a roof over my head.
I don't mind working at the Retching Netch, but I was hoping for a bit more when I moved to Solstheim. Ah well.
There's got to be more to life than sweeping and cleaning all day.
I'll give you this warning only once. Stay out of my way, and we'll do just fine.
If you're dealing with me, there's only one thing to remember. Pay your debts or end up hurt.
I might lend coin to people that need it, but I never lend to strangers. So shove off.
I don't know you, and the last thing I want is a stranger with his nose in my business. Go away.
I don't know you, and the last thing I want is a stranger with her nose in my business. Go away.
I can tell you don't like me. Well guess what. I don't give a damn.
I work for Mogrul. You got a problem, you talk to him.
Mogrul doesn't lend coin to outlanders. Look somewhere else.
You looking for trouble? Stay out of my way.
Haven't you got something better to do?
Get away from me, outlander.
If you're looking for passage, talk to Captain Gjalund.
Talk to Captain Gjalund if you're looking to book passage.
The Northern Maiden is ship-shape and ready to go.
Captain Gjalund does the business around here... I'm just a ship's mate.
Suit yourself.
Casting off!
Right then. Let's be off.
Load up, and we'll get underway.
Get yourself settled, then.
Come back when you actually have the gold.
Well... I guess nothing bad happened the last time. Fine, we'll sail as soon as I get paid.
What?!
That's impossible. Wait... unless she didn't actually die! The heart stone could have kept her right at the edge of life and death.
Could it be? Could Ildari still be alive? Could she be my nemesis?
Wait a moment while I cast a more specific divination.
Ildari? Then it's my own fault. She was my apprentice before Talvas.
She volunteered for one of my experiments involving heart stones.
It was quite annoying when she died.
Hmm. I've known for decades that heart stones will animate the ash.
But it would have to be very close by. Where exactly did you find this heart stone?
Yes, yes. As I was saying, heart stones can animate nearby ash.
Not now. Wait until we are back at Tel Mithryn.
I don't have time to go traipsing all over Solstheim.
If I don't hear back from you after a while, you're probably dead. Maybe I'll send Talvas out next.
The link is weak, so you'll have to be within about a hundred feet of the source.
Maybe not. I hear they've been seen over Skyrim too.
Even so, there is more than enough evidence that someone is out to get me.

A decade ago, this drove him into a deep depression. He shut himself inside his home and refused to come out.

A fine question, and the one that I hear most often from visitors to our town.

Gjalund over at the docks brings me most of my supplies, and the rest comes from bartering with the people in town.

Bah, you're just like the rest of the folks around here.

Lost...
